topic: A person tying a Bowline knot with a thick rope, hands-only close-up.

parsed_generation.script:
[00:00-00:02] Shot 1: [Hands-Only Close-up: loop formation] - Camera: Overhead, Framing: Tight, Distance: Close, Movement: Static, Subject: Hands.
[00:02-00:05] Shot 2: [Hands-Only Close-up: working end through loop and around standing part] - Camera: Side, Framing: Tight, Distance: Close, Movement: Static, Subject: Hands.
[00:05-00:08] Shot 3: [Hands-Only Close-up: tightening bowline to complete knot] - Camera: Overhead, Framing: Tight, Distance: Close, Movement: Slow tracking, Subject: Hands.
parsed_generation.narration: Shot 1 shows hands-only close-up as a thick rope is manipulated into a small loop to start the Bowline. Shot 2 follows the working end as it passes up through the loop, around the standing part, and back down through the loop. In Shot 3, the hands slowly tighten the knot, revealing the completed bowline with the loop secured and the tail free. This sequence captures the precise, hands-on progression required to tie a Bowline knot on a thick rope with only the hands in frame.

feedback: The output stays on topic with hands-only close-ups showing the Bowline steps on a thick rope and preserves a three-shot structure.
